text: I remember this commercial from 2004, the same year that Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas came out.  My friend’s son had been begging and pleading for it but she refused to spend the money.  I said I would buy it for him ONLY if he danced like the old guy with this song playing in front of the store.  We get there and at the last second he backed out.  A father and son had been waiting in the line to buy it (it was pretty long) and I seriously told the dad if his son did the dance instead I’d buy it for him.  The dad told him “You better get your ass over there and do that shit!  You saving me 70 bucks!”  He seriously did and I held up my end.  My friend’s son walked away pissed and empty handed.

text:
First time I ever saw this commercial was summer of '04, I'd just got out of Navy boot and was stationed at Naval Training Center Great Lakes for ATT and ET A school, and I walk into the rec room of my new barracks and like, five dudes are blankly staring at the TV and this comes on.  

Behind them were two sailors playing on a shitty pool table while doing a very poor job of hiding a bottle of Wild Turkey.  

Great memories of that place.  Thank you, Great Lakes.
author: @NeoN-PeoN

text: When I watch the commercial of six flags  besides the old man in the commercial a long time ago who used to own the theme park of six flags which of course he did I am preferring from himself to my uncle Elliott because in real life my uncle used to have hair on his head a long time ago but now that he doesn't in the commercial video that the moment he steps off the bus and the thing that pops up and the music plays he starts to dance funny to impress the people who wants to go to six flags they love his dance he goes come on then he drives the bus they get to six flags he's goes on every ride with the people dance with the people at the park and that's when the commercial ends but I used to go like this to him the moment the music plays I go like this go Elliott then I go uncle Elliott please please don't grab me please which I don't remember what I said but he used to take the joke now he doesn't anymore even though now to this day he is bald wears his glasses to see drives his car not the six flags bus but still whenever he owns the theme park or not he is still a funny old man who dance to impress the people and go to six flags in his bus he takes them there goes on the rides dance all day and he starts to have fun because the old man reminds me of him so no sense of humor from him anymore.

text: @Always Watching when I mean my uncle Elliott who works at six flags that he owns the theme park and that he is in charge of the theme park that not only he drives to the neighborhood to see some people not getting everything done to go to six flags but instead my uncle Elliott on his six flags bus he comes to the neighborhood makes a scene by dancing and that's when I go like this the moment the music is played I go go Elliott go Elliott you got it you got it it's your birthday go Elliott you got it you got it then everybody gets on his bus and he drives them all to six flags then he dances around at the park and that is when the commercial said six flags it's playtime but also my uncle's birthday is September 22nd 1962 but since we are in 2022 he will be 60 years old very soon in the next seven months.

text:
All right, are you ready to edit the video, and  change the tune this guy's dancing to? do this:
1. Start TWO browser windows,
2. one with THIS video, with the sound turned down,
3. one with Maroon 5 "Moves like Jagger" studio version.
4, Stop both, rewind to the beginning, and crank up Moves Like Jagger and start it.
5. Right after Adam finishes saying "just shoot for the stars," start the 6 flags video, and watch.
By Golly, the old guy's got Moves like Jagger...

text: Dig it: Mr. Six appears at the site of a car accident and whisks all involved to Six Flags (including a woman, a man, a cop, and a fatality in a body bag). At one point he dances behind the woman in a very provocative manner, causing the cop to yank him away from her. At the end, Mr. Six begins driving them outta Six Flags, only to cause another fatal accident. As the bus passengers look on at the horrifying results, Mr. Six begins dancing again. The cop gets annoyed and shoots him in the head.

text: Next time have you facts.
author: @ESWlive

text: On March 21, 1999, a 28-year-old woman died, and 10 other guests were injured, when the raft they were on overturned in 2–3 feet of water due to sudden deflation of the air chambers that support the raft
author: @ESWlive

text: On July 26, 1978, three people died when their gondola fell from the cable
author: @ESWlive

text: In July 1984, a 46-year-old woman was riding the Rail Blazer roller coaster when she was flung from the ride and fell 20 feet (6.1 m) to her death.
author: @ESWlive

text: On June 28, 2008, a 17-year-old male from Columbia, South Carolina was decapitated by a passing train after he hopped two six-foot fences and entered a restricted area.
author: @ESWlive

text: On May 26, 2002, a 58-year-old Six Flags foreman was struck in the head and killed by the dangling legs of a passenger after he wandered into the ride's path after entering a locked, no-access area during the ride's operation. The passenger, a 14-year-old girl, was hospitalized with leg injuries and released.[45]
author: @ESWlive

text: On July 10, 2003, a 52-year-old grandmother was strapping her 4-year-old grandson in when the ride started up. She died from blunt-force internal injuries after being struck by a ride vehicle.
author: @ESWlive

text: On May 1, 2004, a 53-year-old, 230 lb man from Bloomfield, Connecticut fell out of his coaster seat during the last turn and was killed.
author: @ESWlive

text: On April 9, 2004, a 21-year-old employee died after being struck by the roller coaster while underneath the track during a test run prior to the park's opening that day.
author: @ESWlive

text: n 1996, a part-time employee was killed while crossing the tracks of the Revolution roller coaster
author: @ESWlive

text: On June 2, 2001, a 28-year-old woman died of a brain aneurysm while riding Goliath
author: @ESWlive

text: On May 3, 2003, an 11-year-old girl from Gary, Indiana collapsed after riding the Raging Bull coaster
author: @ESWlive

text: On May 29, 2004, a 52-year-old ride mechanic from Zion, Illinois was killed by a roller-coaster car as he attempted to cross the tracks
author: @ESWlive

text: On June 17, 1987, a 19-year-old girl died after falling from the Lightnin' Loops shuttle loop roller coaster
author: @ESWlive

text: On May 11, 1984, eight teenage visitors were trapped and killed when the Haunted Castle attraction was destroyed by fire
author: @ESWlive

text: On August 16, 1981, a 20-year-old park employee from Middletown, NJ fell to his death from the Rolling Thunder roller coaster during a routine test run.
author: @ESWlive

text: Want Facts?On July 6, 2012, a 67-year-old employee of the park was killed at Le Vampire. An eyewitness stated that the employee was found underneath the attraction in a restricted area and appeared to have suffered head trauma.
author: @ESWlive

text:
RumbleFighterBowChao ,
He was a well-known Broadway performer. I haven't seen him for 11 years but phoned his best friend this week and he told me about this. I live in Europe and so wasn't familiar with this commercial whatsoever till a few days ago. The dancer's friend (also a highly respectable member of society ) sent me the link to the vid.
author: @Westonelm

text:
RumbleFighterBowChao ,
I respect your opinion and I would have thought the same if I had come across such a comment (like the person on Paul Davidson's blog, who said that the dancer was a teacher in Indiana ). I even hesitated before posting knowing that I would not be believed yet someone somewhere had to tell the truth just once (without revealing all because the dancer wanted to remain anonymous.)
( BTW the makeup took 5 hours.)
author: @Westonelm

author: @Highspeedyv

text:
Paul Davidson claimed falsely (in 2006) that the original Mr Six (here) was Danny Teeson.(bigger build, taller and a longer neck). Teeson didn't even confirm the fact.
The dancer was 58 at the time (I knew him) and he passed away in February of this year (2009) unexpectedly.. Among the 500 people who were packed into an eastern coast church for his funeral were many who knew that he "was" Mr Six and yet the internet brandishes ignorance. Incredible!
